content:
{
  "plan_id": "plugin-management-ui",
  "adversary": "stress",
  "attack_surface": "Validate config edge cases, i18n translation, IPC state races, PluginKind backward compat, loader cache consistency, memory lifecycle, menu trigger wiring",
  "attacks_attempted": ["empty-array-input", "non-array-store-value", "deeply-nested-config", "null-store-grace", "dialog-simultaneous-install-and-manage", "rapid-toggle-race", "preload-api-unavailable", "console-warn-throws", "on-cleanup-missing", "loaded-set-stuck-on-failure", "hmr-listener-leak", "duplicate-plugin-names", "missing-menu-trigger", "switch-label-prop-type", "cancel-button-onClick", "i18n-key-raw-strings", "loader-cache-not-invalidated", "ipc-before-registration-race", "config-type-not-validated", "two-concurrent-installs"],
  "findings": [
    {"severity": "blocker", "description": "All 22 plugin i18n keys rendered as raw literal strings. Dialog title=\"desktop.plugin.manage.title\" renders the key string, not 'Plugin Manager'. TextField labels, Button text, Switch labels, error messages, and data-loss warnings all show raw key identifiers instead of translated text. Neither dialog imports `t()` from './i18n' or `useLanguage()`. Full reproduction: launch app, open Plugin Manager dialog — every text string shows a dotted i18n key.", "repair_instruction": "Add `import { t } from \"./i18n\"` to both dialog-*.tsx files, wrap all 22 \"desktop.plugin.*\" strings with t()"},
    {"severity": "blocker", "description": "Cancel button in dialog-install-plugin.tsx line 143 has NO onClick handler. User cannot dismiss the install dialog except by submitting or reloading the page. The dialog-manage-plugins.tsx inline cancel has correct onClick={cancelInstall}.", "repair_instruction": "Add onClick={cancelInstall} to the Cancel Button in dialog-install-plugin.tsx line 143"},
    {"severity": "blocker", "description": "DesktopPluginProvider (plugin-context.tsx:35-38) creates a loader in onMount but NEVER calls loader.dispose() in onCleanup. Each provider remount (HMR, conditional render) orphans the previous loader with live slot registrations, disposeCallbacks, and in-flight promises. Gradual resource exhaustion under HMR.", "repair_instruction": "Store loader in local variable and add `onCleanup(() => loader.dispose())` inside the onMount callback"},
    {"severity": "blocker", "description": "Menu command 'open-plugin-manager' is handled in index.tsx:365 but never registered in DesktopMenuAction type or DESKTOP_MENU configuration. grep across all packages found only the dispatch handler, no menu item. Users have NO way to open the plugin manager dialog from the native menu.", "repair_instruction": "Add 'open-plugin-manager' to DesktopMenuAction union in packages/app/src/desktop-menu.ts and register a menu item that dispatches this command"},
    {"severity": "major", "description": "Loader cache (this.configs) never invalidated after dialog modifications. ensureConfigs() only reads if this.configs.length === 0. When user toggles/installs/removes plugins via dialog, electron-store updates but loader's cache is stale until full page reload. New plugins never load, removed plugins never dispose.", "repair_instruction": "Add a refreshConfigs() method to DesktopPluginLoader or wire dialog mutations to trigger cache invalidation"},
    {"severity": "major", "description": "Switch component's `label` prop is silently discarded — SwitchProps does not declare a `label` prop (packages/ui/src/components/switch.tsx). It passes through as an unknown HTML attribute on the Kobalte root. No visible 'Enabled'/'Disabled' text renders beside the toggle.", "repair_instruction": "Replace `<Switch label={...} />` with `<Switch>children</Switch>` pattern, wrapping the label text as children"},
    {"severity": "major", "description": "Rapid consecutive toggle lost-update race. Toggle A reads configs() signal snapshot, builds updated array, calls setDesktopPluginConfig. Toggle B reads SAME snapshot before A's IPC response returns, builds different array, overwrites A's write. First toggle's change silently reverted.", "repair_instruction": "Add a compare-and-swap pattern: read current store state before each write, or serialize operations through a local queue"},
    {"severity": "major", "description": "Transient IPC failure causes permanent empty plugin state. If getDesktopPluginConfig IPC fails during initial load (preload delay, race), .catch(() => undefined) silently swallows the error. No retry, no error UI, no user indication. Recovery requires hard page refresh.", "repair_instruction": "Add retry logic with backoff or surface IPC errors to the user with a 'Retry' affordance"},
    {"severity": "major", "description": "Plugin stuck in `loaded` set on import failure. `this.loaded.add(entry.name)` executes BEFORE `await import(entry.path)`. If dynamic import throws, name remains in loaded Set permanently — plugin can never be retried until dispose().", "repair_instruction": "Move loaded.add after successful desktop() call, or add try/catch that removes from loaded on failure"},
    {"severity": "major", "description": "Store returns non-array (string, number, object, undefined) → validateAndFilterPluginConfigs silently returns { configs: [], dropped: 0 }. All existing plugin data silently lost with zero user feedback. No console.warn for non-array input.", "repair_instruction": "Add a console.error/warn for non-array input before returning empty, and consider throwing or logging to Sentry"},
    {"severity": "major", "description": "Duplicate plugin name entries are accepted. If electron-store has two entries with same name (from concurrent race or store corruption), both pass validation. The second entry is unreachable through UI toggle/remove (uses find() which finds first only).", "repair_instruction": "Add deduplication in validateAndFilterPluginConfigs and duplicate detection in the handlers"},
    {"severity": "major", "description": "Install + toggle race: submitInstall uses stale local configs() to build updated array. If toggle happens concurrently, submit's setDesktopPluginConfig overwrites toggle's changes (or vice versa). Newly installed plugin silently lost.", "repair_instruction": "Have submitInstall re-fetch configs via getDesktopPluginConfig() before building the updated array (as the standalone dialog-install-plugin does)"},
    {"severity": "major", "description": "Optional `config` field in PluginConfigEntry is not type-validated by isValidPluginConfigEntry. A config of type string/number/array passes validation. Downstream plugin code accessing `config[key]` would crash on non-object config.", "repair_instruction": "Add `typeof obj.config === 'object' || obj.config === undefined` validation in isValidPluginConfigEntry"},
    {"severity": "major", "description": "onMenuCommand and onDeepLink listeners registered at module scope with cleanup function discarded. Under HMR, unbounded listener accumulation. After 100 HMR cycles, 100 duplicate listeners fire per menu command.", "repair_instruction": "Move listener registration inside render() callback's onMount with onCleanup, or store cleanup functions at module scope"},
    {"severity": "major", "description": "isValidPluginConfigEntry accepts empty string for name and path (typeof check passes). import('') throws module-not-found error. Plugin with empty name/path gets saved to store but fails to load.", "repair_instruction": "Add min-length validation: typeof obj.name === 'string' && obj.name.length > 0"},
    {"severity": "major", "description": "Dynamic import failure in loadAll() (Promise.all) rejects the entire aggregated promise — all other plugins fail to load. One bad plugin DoSes the whole plugin system.", "repair_instruction": "Use Promise.allSettled instead of Promise.all in loadAll(), or wrap each loadEntry in try/catch with error logging"},
    {"severity": "minor", "description": "dialog-install-plugin.tsx is dead code — never imported anywhere. The actual install flow is entirely inside dialog-manage-plugins.tsx view mode switching. The standalone component adds build weight but is never rendered.", "repair_instruction": "Either wire it into the UI (preferred) or remove it to avoid dead-code confusion"},
    {"severity": "minor", "description": "i18n key 'desktop.plugin.config.invalidEntries' with {{count}} template is defined in en.ts but never referenced in any component. Dialog uses 'desktop.plugin.config.dataLossWarning' instead (generic message without count).", "repair_instruction": "Replace the generic dataLossWarning with invalidEntries using resolved count from dropped() signal"},
    {"severity": "minor", "description": "15 non-English locale files lack all 22 plugin i18n keys. Plugin UI shows English text for non-EN locales. Functional (falls back to base dict) but inconsistent — menu/updater/CLI translations appear in the locale but plugin strings stay English.", "repair_instruction": "Add translations to all locale files, or document as intentional MVP shortcut"},
    {"severity": "minor", "description": "Missing onCleanup for in-flight IPC calls. If dialog unmounts while loadConfigs() IPC is in flight, signal setConfigs() fires on unmounted component. Dialog remount shows flash of stale data.", "repair_instruction": "Add an abort controller or cancellation token to loadConfigs() lifecycle"},
    {"severity": "minor", "description": "setDesktopPluginConfig drops invalid entries but dialog only checks dropped on get, not on set. User toggling a plugin doesn't see data-loss warnings for any entries that were silently dropped during the write.", "repair_instruction": "Check dropped > 0 in toggleEnabled/removePlugin result handling as well"},
    {"severity": "informational", "description": "English fallback for non-EN locales via base dict merge works correctly — no crash or TypeScript error for missing locale keys."},
    {"severity": "informational", "description": "13 automated bun tests for isValidPluginConfigEntry and validateAndFilterPluginConfigs all pass (verified by running bun test). Tests cover success and failure paths comprehensively."},
    {"severity": "informational", "description": "PluginKind union now includes 'desktop' (shared.ts:37). readV1Plugin validates desktop() entrypoint with 3-way mutual exclusion."},
    {"severity": "informational", "description": "reserved store name 'desktop-plugin-config' correctly blocks generic store-get/set/delete IPC handlers — isolation works as intended."}
  ],
  "verdict": "falsified_blocking"
}
